content.title: [BUG] LLM Hallucination of Non-Existent Node IDs in Edge Generation
content.excerpt:
### Description
The neural subsystem (`call_llm_policy`) is responsible for proposing structural updates to the graph. While the symbolic engine strictly handles validation, under complex situational inputs, the LLM occasionally hallucinates `source` or `target` identifiers inside the `add_edges` payload that do not exist in the current active graph state.

### Impact
When a node ID is hallucinated, the `add_edge()` strict guardrail catches it and quietly drops the edge:
```python
if edge.get("s
